CREATE TABLE form_params (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value VARCHAR(255));
SELECT http_param('username') FROM form_params WHERE id = 1;
SELECT http_param('password') FROM form_params WHERE id = 2;
SELECT http_param('email') AS user_email, http_param('username') AS user_name FROM form_params WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM form_params WHERE name = 'username');
CREATE TABLE user_auth (user_id INTEGER, auth_code VARCHAR(255), FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES form_params(id));
SELECT u.user_id, http_param('username') FROM user_auth u JOIN form_params f ON u.user_id = f.id WHERE f.name = 'username';
SELECT http_param('non_existing') AS non_existing_param, http_param() AS all_params FROM form_params;
CREATE TABLE http_logs (log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, request_param VARCHAR(255), log_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT log_id, request_param FROM http_logs WHERE request_param LIKE '%test%';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT http_param() AS param FROM form_params WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
